event: Scene from the Life of Christ (Stained Glass Detail)
symbols: prayer shawl (tallit) with blue stripes — Jewish religious devotion and covenant identity
white head covering — reverence and piety
aged, dignified face — wisdom and the weight of prophetic expectation
description: A detail from a stained-glass window showing the face and upper body of an elderly bearded man wearing a white head covering and a prayer shawl with prominent blue stripes. His expression is solemn and contemplative, with deep-set eyes gazing slightly to the side. A second older figure is partially visible at the lower left. The glass work is detailed and realistic, with the lead lines visible between glass sections.
theological_description: This stained-glass detail depicts a Jewish elder or religious leader, likely from a scene in the life of Christ. The prayer shawl (tallit) with its blue fringes (tzitzit) reflects the commandment of Numbers 15:38-40, where God instructs the Israelites to make tassels on the corners of their garments with a blue cord, to remember and obey all the Lord’s commandments. The elder’s dignified bearing may represent one of the faithful who awaited the consolation of Israel (Luke 2:25), or a Pharisee or member of the Sanhedrin who encountered Jesus during his ministry. The stained-glass medium itself embodies the theology of divine light passing through material creation to illuminate the faithful within the church.

scripture_refs.reason: The commandment to wear fringes with blue cords, visible in the prayer shawl depicted
The righteous and devout who waited for the consolation of Israel

_meta.title: Life of Christ. Eglise Saint-Sulpice de Fougères (detail)
